Senna rubiginosa is a plant species in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as a flowering plant with medicinal properties. The leaves and pods of Senna rubiginosa have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including constipation and diarrhea, due to their laxative properties. The bioactive constituents of Senna rubiginosa include sennosides, anthraquinones, and other glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Senna rubiginosa are teas, capsules, and extracts, often used to support digestive health and relieve constipation.
